summary: Example handoff for a future mobile FPS vertical slice.
observations: The repo has a clean Three.js FPS foundation, but the first gameplay vertical slice has not started.
The first slice should prove mobile landscape controls, nonblank WebGL canvas, one arena, one enemy, one weapon, and one reward choice.
decisions: Asset playground validation is required before importing external GLB assets into gameplay.
Visible-browser 60 FPS is the target; headless smoke should assert state and budgets.
risks: External asset licenses can block redistribution if not checked before download.
Touch controls can overlap HUD or fail multitouch if not designed before gameplay tuning.
